title: STUDY TOUR | Blue Education

main_content:
STUDY TOURS

The page features two main study tour programmes:

1. BUDDY PROGRAMME - A programme for students to experience Australian school life with local buddies

2. ENGLISH & CULINARY PROGRAMME - Combines English learning with culinary experiences

Note: This is a landing page that links to the more detailed /study-tours (Buddy Programme) page.

title: BUDDY PROGRAMME | Blue Education
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured (ID: ss_9131iwflb) - page shows BUDDY PROGRAMME header with programme details
main_content:
BUDDY PROGRAMME

Did you know?

Australia has some of the best colleges in the world. If you look at the list of the top 100 in the world, you will find that seven of them are actually found in Australia, while the country only has 23 million people!

You also have a lot of choice when you come to Australia. There are over 1100 institutions found across the entire country.

The cost of living in Australia is actually great, especially when compared to the United Kingdom and United States.

Making new friends

The Buddy Programme is an amazing and highly engaging journey that can develop the student's language, social and communication skills.

The programme runs for fourteen days. Students attend school with their Buddies and live with homestay families.

You can trust our programme

Our programme runs with an established and long trusting relationship with the Anglican Schools and the homestay families within the local community. Our schools are sprawled on huge campus grounds, surrounded by luscious greenery, beautiful wildlife with the luxury of proximity to the glorious Indian Ocean. We offer structured English lessons and discussion topics surrounding historical, cultural and geographical themes of Australia. Our students are placed with homestay families who provide a warm and safe environment to care for their needs. Our programme places emphasis on cognitive and social development opportunities through classroom and field activities.

The Homestay Experience

Homestay is the quickest way for a student to immerse in their host's culture. Homestay families have to pass background clearance checks by the police and interviews by our local representative office.

Homestay families provide for the meals of the student as well as emotional support and needs during the entire programme. The Student gets to participate in all the family activities of the host families during weekends.

The High School Experience

English is taught in class in the mornings. English classes run on several themes based on the Australian population, geographical and cultural settings (i.e. wildlife, interesting places, popular activities and way of life). Lessons are structured in order to promote the usage and understanding of the language.

Integration is in the afternoon when Students get to join in the routine of the high school students. All subjects such as Science and Math are taught in English with the exception of foreign language classes.

Local Field Trip is integrated into the classroom studies to elevate the interest and understanding of the lessons taught.

Students will get the opportunity to learn through the sights, sounds and taste of the rich cultural diversity of the Australian ways. To name a few places would be Caversham Wildlife Park, King's Park, Fremantle Market, Maritime & Historical Walk, Penguin and Sea Lion Cruise, Rottnest Island, the Pinnacles, sandboarding adventure.

We can also make special arrangements with the local councils to participate in voluntary work. This would look great for the student's CV when he or she is applying for scholarships or university entry programmes in the future.

title: CORE SERVICES | Blue Education

CORE SERVICES

This is our dedication to work with you towards a desired pathway for a successful career.

EDUCATION

No matter where you come from, we shall endeavour to understand your needs and strive to fulfil your learning goals and career aspirations!

- Course and school selection/ enrolment/ changes
- School transfer/ withdrawal

MIGRATION

Our team is professional and honest and will always be transparent with you. Put your anxieties aside and appoint one of our Registered Migration Agent as your personal representative. Our agent will help clear your doubts and manage your expectations and the process from preliminary assessment to the final approval for your entry into Australia.

Our service delivery:
- Skilled Migration
- Graduate Work Visa
- Post Study Work Visa
- Sponsored Skilled Visa
- Employer's Standard Business Sponsorship
- Family Migration
- Spouse / De-Facto / Child Visa

CAREER

Our Career Counsellors come with years of corporate experience and are eager to share with you their experience and quick tips in finding your niche in the job marketplace. Every graduate desires for that dream job, along with hundreds, if not, thousands out there. Let us help you make the difference, and be the difference!

Our counselors are ready to listen to you and help you unleash your potentials. We help new migrants, graduates or current job seekers who are looking at career options, or even considering a career change and thus require advice in planning and execution.

Our service delivery:
- Career counselling
- Resume review and construction
- Interview coaching
- Job search strategies
- Cultural adaptation initiatives

title: TOEFL | Blue Education
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured (ID: ss_56394779n) - page shows TOEFL iBT Test announcement with group of diverse students
main_content:
TOEFL iBT Test - Now Available at Blue Education

We're proud to announce that Blue Education is now an authorised TOEFL iBT ETS authorised test centre!

The TOEFL iBT Test is one of the most widely respected English language proficiency exams in the world. The test is accepted by over 13,000 universities, institutions, and professional registration bodies in over 160 countries. TOEFL iBT is accepted by the Department of Home Affairs for various visa applications, including student, temporary graduate (485), and skilled migration visas.

TOEFL iBT Test has a fair scoring system and enhanced digital experience so it has been a preferred choice for students aiming for international study, skilled migration, or career advancement. It opens doors to global opportunities by providing a clear, reliable measure of English proficiency for academic and visa purposes.

At Blue Education, you can now sit for your TOEFL iBT test in a secure, comfortable, and professional testing environment right here in the heart of Perth.

WHY TOEFL iBT:

1. Globally Respected & Widely Accepted
Whether it's for university admission, visa applications, or professional registration, the TOEFL iBT is the test institutions and governments trust - including the Australian Department of Home Affairs.

2. Faster Registration & More Flexibility
Register quickly and easily with improved scheduling tools and local payment options (in select countries). Plus, you can retake the test in as little as 3 days if needed. See your unofficial Reading and Listening scores immediately after your test, and know exactly when to expect your full official results, typically available in just 4-8 days.

3. Free Practice Tools to Help You Succeed
Access a full-length practice test, personalised study plan, daily prep activities, and even a 40-minute sample test to get familiar with the real testing environment.

4. Shorter Test Time
The total test duration is now under 2 hours, making it faster and more efficient while still thoroughly assessing your English skills.

5. Instant & Clear Score Insights
See your unofficial Reading and Listening scores immediately after your test, and know exactly when to expect your full official results, typically available in just 4-8 days.

TOEFL iBT opens doors to top universities worldwide

Access to Free TOEFL TestReady
No other English language test provider has a prep offering like this - designed for you, with you. TOEFL TestReady combines the best TOEFL iBT prep offerings with exclusive features and deeper insights to enhance your English communication skills. All feedback, recommendations, personalized insights and tips are developed by the same teams that write and produce the TOEFL iBT test.

title: WHY AUSTRALIA | Blue Education
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured (ID: ss_4993ryxeo) - page shows WHY AUSTRALIA header with Perth city skyline image
main_content:
WHY AUSTRALIA

Australia is a popular destination for international students with its highly livable cities, temperate climate, a strong economy, an amiable culture and high-quality education system.

Home to some of the world's leading universities, you have the freedom to choose a path that suits your particular goals. There are thousands of courses out there. Whichever course you choose, you will certainly experience a unique kind of learning!

Times, CNN, Economist are amongst the many who have proudly reminded us that Australia does have many liveable cities within its fortress of sound economy, exceptional education institutions and multicultural brilliance. Australia has some of the best colleges in the world. Look at the list of colleges in the top 100 countries in the world, 7 are actually in Australia. Amazing, since Australia has only 23 million residents.

You also have a lot of choices when you come to Australia. There are over 1100 institutions found across the entire country. The cost of living in Australia is great, especially when compared to the UK and the USA.

Here are top 5 reasons why Australia:

1. GLOBAL RECOGNITION
Schools and employers around the world recognise degrees from Australian schools. Graduates from Australia are highly sought because of the impressive international reputation of the Australian education system, carefully regulated by the Australian government to maintain high standards.

2. DIVERSITY IN EDUCATION
Institutions in Australia offer a wide variety of courses and degrees, so international students can easily find the school and field that are right for them. Depending on their interest, students can choose between universities, vocational education, and English language training. It is also easy for students to move between qualification levels or institutions.

3. TECHNOLOGY
It is most appealing to international students because of the quality of scientific research in Australia. Australia is at the forefront of new technology and innovations. Students who study in Australia can take advantage of the country's impressive technology and research resources.

4. JOB OPPORTUNITIES
While the UK closes doors for doctors and the US focuses more on IT professionals, Australia offers more job facilities in multiple disciplines. It can be agriculture or aeronautics, accounts or actuaries. With the Subclass 485, Post Study Work Visa, would allow 2 to 4 years of work opportunities.

5. AUSTRALIA IS SIMPLY TOO BEAUTIFUL TO RESIST!
The white sandy beaches, the blue skies, the crisp air. How can anyone NOT fall in love with Australia! There is not a moment of dullness in this beautiful country. Abundance of opportunities!

So, what are you waiting for?

Let us journey with you to a discovery of a lifetime! Australia is for YOU!

title: ZERO TOLERANCE | Blue Education
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured (ID: ss_0114g4eul) - page shows ZERO TOLERANCE TOWARDS ABUSE POLICY header with silhouette image
main_content:
ZERO TOLERANCE TOWARDS ABUSE POLICY

At Blue Education, we are committed to providing a safe and respectful environment for all our employees. We believe in fostering a workplace culture that promotes professionalism, mutual respect, and dignity. As part of this commitment, we have implemented a Zero Tolerance Towards Abusive Behaviour Policy, which applies to all customers and clients interacting with our employees or accessing our services. Abusive behavior includes, but is not limited to, verbal, physical, or emotional harassment, threats, discrimination, or any form of disrespectful conduct.

Definition of Abusive Behavior:
Abusive behavior encompasses any action, language, or gesture that is intended to intimidate, belittle, threaten, discriminate against, or harm the physical or emotional well-being of our employees. This includes, but is not limited to, the following behaviors:
- Verbal and written abuse, such as yelling, using profane language, or making derogatory comments in written forms.
- Physical aggression, such as assault, pushing, or any form of physical harm.
- Sexual harassment or inappropriate advances.
- Discrimination based on race, gender, religion, age, disability, nationality, or any other protected characteristic.
- Threats of violence or harm, whether explicit or implied.
- Cyberbullying or online harassment through any digital platform.
- Any other behavior that is deemed abusive or harmful by the company.

Reporting Process:
Employees who experience or witness abusive behavior from a customer or client are encouraged to report it immediately to their supervisor, manager, or the designated HR representative. The company will provide clear channels of communication and maintain confidentiality throughout the reporting process. Employees will not face any negative consequences or retaliation for reporting incidents of abusive behavior.

Investigation and Consequences:
Upon receiving a report of abusive behavior, the company will promptly investigate the incident. This may involve collecting evidence, speaking to witnesses, and consulting with the parties involved. The company will take appropriate action based on the severity and nature of the behavior, which may include:
- Issuing a warning or formal notice to the customer or client.
- Restricting or terminating the customer's access to our services.
- Involving legal authorities if the behavior constitutes a criminal offense.
- Pursuing civil remedies against the offending party, if necessary.

Communication of Policy:
The Zero Tolerance Towards Abusive Behavior Policy will be communicated to all customers and clients through various means, including but not limited to:
- Displaying signs or notices in public areas and online platforms.
- Including a statement in service agreements or terms of use.
- Communicating the policy during customer interactions through email, phone, or in-person meetings.
- Providing educational materials or training sessions to raise awareness about respectful conduct.

Continuous Improvement:
We are committed to continuously improving our policies and procedures to ensure the safety and well-being of our employees. We will review and update this policy as needed, taking into account feedback from our employees and evolving industry best practices.

By implementing this Zero Tolerance Towards Abusive Behaviour Policy, we strive to create a workplace environment that fosters respect, professionalism, and inclusivity. We believe that every individual deserves to be treated with dignity and will not tolerate any form of abusive behavior towards our employees.

title: What Happens When a Dependant Turns 18 or 24 on an Australian Visa?
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured (ID: ss_60145t9o2) - blog post dated Nov 24, 2025, 3 min read
main_content:
What Happens When a Dependant Turns 18 or 24 on an Australian Visa?

A Guide for Families Navigating Temporary and Permanent Visa Pathways

For many international families living, studying, or working in Australia, visa requirements can feel complex - especially when children on dependent visas reach key age milestones.

Turning 18 or 24 can affect a young person's eligibility to remain attached to a parent's visa, which may require families to take action sooner than expected.

This guide breaks down what happens at each age, what families need to prepare, and how to plan ahead to avoid unexpected visa issues.

Turning 18 on a Temporary Visa (e.g., as a Dependant on a Student Visa)

When a dependant turns 18, Australian immigration law begins to treat them as an adult. That means, to remain on a parent's Student Visa, they must still meet the official definition of a dependent child.

What Dependants Aged 18+ Must Demonstrate:
To stay attached to a parent's temporary visa, dependants over 18 typically must be:
- Financially dependent on their parent(s)
- Not married, engaged, or in a de facto relationship
- Studying (usually full-time)
- Reliant on their parent(s) for their day-to-day living costs

Evidence is important - immigration may ask for documentation showing that the young adult is genuinely supported by their parents.

What Families Should Do When a Dependant Turns 18:
To ensure a smooth transition into adulthood while maintaining visa eligibility, consider the following steps:

1. Update Documentation - Ensure your dependant has an up-to-date passport, enrolment confirmation, and proof of financial support.

2. Maintain Appropriate Health Cover - Dependants aged 18 and over must have valid OSHC (or equivalent) at all times.

3. Prepare for Future Applications - If the family will be applying for a new visa, ensure the 18-year-old continues to meet dependency requirements and can be included.

4. Plan Early for Independence - If the young adult becomes financially independent, enters a relationship, or finishes study, they may need to apply for their own visa.

Turning 18 doesn't automatically remove a dependant from a family visa - but it does require careful attention to ensure all requirements are still met.

What Families Should Do When a Dependant Turns 18 on a Student Visa?
If the Dependant wishes to remain in Australia, we will need to explore the option in which the Dependant can apply as the Main Applicant and this would depend on the intention of remaining in Australia. If the Dependant wishes to study or continue to study, then the Dependant will need to apply for his/her own student visa.

Turning 24 While Included in a Parent's Permanent Visa Application

Turning 24 is one of the most important (and often overlooked) milestones for dependants included in a parent's permanent residency (PR) application.

Under Australian migration rules:
- Children under 18 can be included as dependants
- Young adults 18 to 23 can also be included if they are still financially dependent and studying
- Children 24 and over can only be included in very limited circumstances - generally only if they have a disability that prevents them from supporting themselves

Why This Matters:
If a parent has applied for PR and their child turns 24 before the visa is granted, the child may:
- No longer meet the definition of a dependant
- Be removed from the application
- Need to apply for a separate visa to remain in Australia lawfully

This can be distressing for families who were expecting to receive PR together. The best way to avoid complications is to plan early - ideally well before the child turns 23.

There is certainly a lot to consider in each and every circumstance of the family. If you wish to speak to a trained and licensed professional on your options, just reach out to our team at Blue Education +61411708899 | info@blueeducation.com.au

title: Your Guide to Australia's Group of Eight Universities: How to Get In and Why It's Worth It
screenshot_note: Screenshot captured (ID: ss_4590bmske) - blog post dated Nov 12, 2025 (Updated Nov 18, 2025), 3 min read
main_content:
Your Guide to Australia's Group of Eight Universities: How to Get In and Why It's Worth It

If you're considering studying in Australia and aiming for a world-class qualification, the prestigious Group of Eight (Go8) universities offer some of the best opportunities available. In this article we'll walk you through how to apply to Go8 universities, what a degree from them can do for your career and life, and what you need to prepare (including costs, documents and planning). Whether you're an international student or a domestic applicant, this guide will give you a clear roadmap.

1. What Are the Group of Eight Universities?

Before diving into the application process, it's useful to understand what we mean by the Group of Eight. These are eight leading Australian research-intensive universities known for their high academic standards, global rankings and rich student experience. Attending one of these universities can give you the reputation and research opportunities that carry weight internationally - a smart investment if you're serious about your future.

2. How to Apply: Step-by-Step for International Students

Here is a typical application process when applying to a Go8 university:
- Step 1: Choose your course & check entry requirements
- Step 2: Prepare your documents
- Step 3: Submit the application
- Step 4: Accept offer & apply for student visa
- Step 5: Obtain a student visa grant, arrive, enrol and start your studies

These would be some of the documents you need to be preparing beforehand:
- Academic Records - Transcripts, degree certificates, grading scale.
- English Language Test Scores - IELTS, TOEFL, PTE, or Cambridge.
- Identification - Passport (and birth certificate if needed).
- Statement of Purpose / Personal Statement as to why you would like to pursue this programme
- Curriculum Vitae (CV) / Resume
- Letters of Recommendation - 2-3 referees (is an added advantage)
- Research Proposal - applicable for research/Master of Philosophy or PhD programs.
- Financial Documents - proof of funds, tuition fee receipt.
- Additional (if applicable) - portfolio, work experience letters, medical reports.

3. Rough Costs & What to Budget For

It's useful to have ballpark figures so you can plan your budget. Tuition fees range from AUD 35,000-55,000 per year, while living costs average around AUD 30,000-35,000 annually. You need to plan for your tuition fees, visa cost, health cover, and travel costs as well.

4. What You Need to Prepare (Beyond Finances)

Here are the key things to have ready so your application and move go smoothly: English proficiency tests (IELTS/TOEFL), academic transcripts, personal statement, visa application, health cover, accommodation and early preparation (6-9 months ahead). If you intend to pursue an MBA in selected universities, you will also need to prepare for the GMAT.

5. What Can a Degree from a Go8 University Do for You?

Choosing a degree from one of the Group of Eight universities can open doors - here's how:
- Global recognition & reputation
- Strong industry connections & research opportunities
- Enhanced career outcomes & pathways
- Skills you'll gain

So, you know you can unlock a future that can exceed your expectations when you study and graduate from a Go8 university in Australia. Universities like University of Melbourne, UNSW Sydney, and Australian National University are globally recognized for academic excellence, research-led teaching, and state-of-the-art facilities. A Go8 degree doesn't just offer world-class education - it boosts your career prospects, connects you with industry leaders, and opens doors to international opportunities. For ambitious students, these universities provide the perfect platform to access scholarships, professional networks, and real-world internships, giving you a competitive edge in today's global job market.

Studying at a Go8 university is more than just academics - it's a life-changing experience. With vibrant campus life, innovative learning environments, and dedicated support for international students, you'll thrive both personally and professionally. Graduates of Go8 universities are highly sought-after by employers worldwide, reflecting their emphasis on graduate employability and cutting-edge research.

At Blue Education, we help you navigate the application process, making your dream of studying at a top Australian university a reality.

Applying to one of Australia's top Group of Eight universities is a big step - and a potentially life-changing one. With the right preparation (course choice, documents, budget, timeline) and a clear understanding of what a degree can offer you (career outcomes, global recognition, valuable skills), you'll be well equipped to make the most of the experience.

Start your journey to a prestigious degree and a global career today! If you wish to speak to a trained and experienced education counsellor on your choices, you can reach out via WhatsApp +61411708899 | Email: info@blueeducation.com.au
